# Delivery Notifications

> Send automated SMS and email notifications to customers and drivers at each order status change

**Category:** Notification · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** fleet · notifications · sms · email · customer · driver · alerts

## What this does

Send automated SMS and email notifications to customers and drivers at each order status change

Specifies 6 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **notification_id** *(text, required)* — Notification ID
- **order_uuid** *(text, required)* — Order
- **recipient_type** *(select, required)* — Recipient Type
- **recipient_identifier** *(text, required)* — Recipient Email or Phone
- **channel** *(select, required)* — Delivery Channel
- **template_id** *(text, optional)* — Message Template
- **subject** *(text, optional)* — Email Subject
- **message** *(rich_text, required)* — Message Body
- **trigger_event** *(text, required)* — Trigger Event
- **sent_at** *(datetime, optional)* — Sent At
- **delivered_at** *(datetime, optional)* — Delivered At
- **status** *(select, required)* — Status

## What must be true

- **status_change_triggers:** Notifications are triggered automatically on order status changes: created, dispatched, en_route, arrived, completed, cancelled
- **template_required:** Each notification type (email/SMS) must have a corresponding template configured
- **no_pii_in_messages:** Sensitive data (PII, internal IDs) must never be included in notification messages
- **retry_policy:** Failed notifications are retried up to 3 times with exponential backoff
- **valid_contact:** Customers must have a valid email address or phone number to receive notifications
- **opt_out_respected:** Notification opt-out preferences must be respected; never send to opted-out contacts
- **sms_length:** SMS messages must comply with carrier length limits (160 characters for standard SMS)
- **audit_log:** Each notification is logged with status for delivery audit trails
- **asynchronous:** Notification sending is asynchronous and must not block order status transitions
- **template_variables:** Message templates support variable substitution for order ID, customer name, ETA, etc.

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Notification Sent On Dispatch** — when order.dispatched event received; customer has a valid email or phone number; customer has not opted out of notifications, then Dispatch notification queued for customer.
- **Notification Sent On Completion** — when order.completed event received; customer has a valid contact, then Delivery completion notification queued for customer.
- **Driver Notified On Dispatch** — when dispatch.assigned event received; driver has a registered mobile device, then Driver notified of new order assignment via push or SMS.
- **Notification Delivered** — when provider confirms delivery, then Notification delivery confirmed.

**❌ Failure paths**

- **Recipient Opted Out** — when customer has opted out of notifications, then Notification suppressed — recipient has opted out. *(error: `NOTIFICATION_OPT_OUT`)*
- **Notification Failed** — when provider reports failure; retry count has reached maximum, then Notification failed after maximum retries. *(error: `NOTIFICATION_DELIVERY_FAILED`)*

## Errors it can return

- `NOTIFICATION_DELIVERY_FAILED` — We were unable to deliver the notification. Please check your contact details.
- `NOTIFICATION_OPT_OUT` — Notification not sent — recipient has opted out.
- `NOTIFICATION_MISSING_CONTACT` — No valid contact information found for this recipient.
- `NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_NOT_FOUND` — Notification template not configured for this event.

## Connects to

- **order-lifecycle** *(required)* — Order status changes trigger notification events
- **fleet-customer-contacts** *(required)* — Customer contact information for notification delivery
- **driver-profile** *(recommended)* — Driver contact info for dispatch notifications
